name: Allied Cash Advance
description_short: Allied Cash Advance offers installment loans and cash advances at physical locations, with same-day or next-day funding available. They serve customers who need quick access to emergency funds.
description_long:
Allied Cash Advance is a storefront lender operating at least one Mesa, Arizona location (2706 East University Drive, Suite F9) that provides short-term lending products. The company has been in the emergency lending space long enough to establish a retail footprint and maintain customer service operations, as evidenced by their physical location and established phone support line (1-800-528-1974).

Their core offerings include installment loans (unsecured personal loans repaid on a fixed schedule), cash advances, and the Netspend Prepaid Mastercard® as a secondary financial product. They also offer retail remote refinancing and reloading options for existing customers. The company accepts applications through three channels: online, in-store, and by phone (where available). Funding timelines are competitive—they advertise same-day funding if using a debit card in-store and next-business-day funding for online applications.

Allied Cash Advance distinguishes itself through strong local customer reviews (4.9 stars from 377 Google reviews on the Mesa location page) and emphasis on staff quality, with multiple reviews specifically praising individual employees by name. They market flexibility in credit considerations, stating that while credit is a factor in underwriting, bad or no credit will not automatically disqualify applicants. The company also operates a reloadable prepaid card product, expanding beyond pure lending.

However, the company carries inherent limitations of the emergency lending category. Their website provides no transparency on APR, fees, loan terms, or maximum amounts—critical information for evaluating cost. As an installment lender targeting underbanked consumers, they likely charge rates significantly higher than traditional banks. The website was temporarily down during this research, suggesting potential system reliability concerns. No information is provided about state licensing, regulatory oversight, or complaint history.
pros: Same-day funding available in-store using debit card; next-business-day for online applications
4.9-star Google rating from 377 verified customer reviews on Mesa location
Multiple application channels: in-store, online, and phone (where available)
Accepts applicants with bad or no credit; credit is a factor but not automatic disqualifier
Physical storefront location with stated hours (Monday-Saturday 10am-6pm, Saturday 10am-2pm)
Offers retail remote refinancing and reloading for existing customers
Clear application requirements posted upfront (no hidden documentation surprises)
Secondary product offering (Netspend prepaid card) expands financial services beyond lending
cons: Website provides zero transparency on APR, fees, loan amounts, or repayment terms—critical for cost comparison
Website was down for 'system maintenance' during research, raising reliability concerns
No information about state licensing, regulatory compliance, or complaint history provided
As an emergency lender, almost certainly charges rates substantially higher than traditional banks or credit unions
Limited geographic footprint evidence; only one Mesa location verified; unclear if multi-state operator
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers in Mesa, AZ needing same-day emergency cash
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who've been rejected by traditional lenders
Existing customers refinancing or reloading current loans with the company
Those willing to pay premium rates for speed and minimal credit requirements
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best for Mesa-area residents needing immediate cash ($100-$1K range based on category) who cannot qualify for traditional loans and are willing to pay premium rates for same-day funding. The primary caveat is complete lack of pricing transparency—applicants should request full APR and fee disclosure before accepting any loan terms, as emergency lenders in this category typically charge 300%+ APR.
services: Installment loans (unsecured personal loans, fixed repayment schedule)
Retail remote reloans for existing customers
Retail remote refinancing for existing customers
In-store customer service and loan counseling
